steps.title: Identify the greatest common factor
steps.content: Every term in \(17x^3 + 2x^2 + 7x\) contains at least one factor of \(x\). The GCF is \(x\).

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The result \(x(17x^2 + 2x + 7)\) matches choice C exactly.

explanation:
• Choice A — 'x(x² + 2x + 7)': Expanding gives x³ + 2x² + 7x, missing the coefficient 17 on the cubic term. ✗
  • Choice B — 'x(17x³ + 2x² + 1)': Expanding gives 17x⁴ + 2x³ + x, which changes the degree and loses the 7x term. ✗
  • Choice C — 'x(17x² + 2x + 7)': Expanding gives 17x³ + 2x² + 7x, which is exactly the original expression. ✓
  • Choice D — '26(x³ + x² + x)': Expanding gives 26x³ + 26x² + 26x; the coefficients 17, 2, 7 are gone and replaced by 26. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
